ip_outcome_note: Confirmed via card name ('Vivi'), job ('Black Mage'), category (Final Fantasy IX), and canonical visual identifiers: enormous drooping wide-brimmed pointed hat (Vivi's signature design quirk vs. standard black mages), glowing circular eye-lights beneath brim, layered robes. IP is Square Enix-internal (Final Fantasy IX). No external IP risk.

trivia_bullets.text: Vivi Ornitier is the black mage protagonist of Final Fantasy IX (PlayStation, 2000). His defining visual identifiers — oversized drooping pointed hat, glowing yellow/orange circular eyes beneath the brim, layered robes — are present in this card's art. Unlike standard Black Mage soldiers in FF IX, Vivi's hat droops down his back rather than pointing upright, marking him as a longer-lived prototype distinct from the manufactured mage-soldiers.

Opus VIII was released in 2019, the eighth main FFTCG expansion. Rarity: Hero (H) — one tier below Legend, used for significant named characters. Cost 1, Fire element, Power 2000, Black Mage job, Category IX.
sources: WebSearch: Square Enix FFTCG Opus VIII release page

trivia_bullets.text: When Vivi enters the field, choose 1 opposing Forward and deal it 2000 damage. If you control 4 or more Category IX Characters, deal 7000 instead. If you control 7 or more Category IX Characters, deal 10000 instead. Scalable enter-the-battlefield damage that rewards building a full Final Fantasy IX party. At max threshold, a 1-cost card deals 10000 damage on entry — exceptional rate for the cost.
sources: WebSearch: Final Fantasy TCG Opus VIII Vivi card text (via multiple retailer listings)

trivia_bullets.text: Art style is highly stylized and painterly, consistent with Yoshitaka Amano's illustration register — spindly figure, trailing ribbon-elements, pale textured wash background. Amano has contributed art to multiple FFTCG Hero and Legend-tier cards across the Opus series. Artist credit for 8-016H is not confirmed in available sources; flag for verification against physical card credit.

Vivi's existential arc — discovering he is a manufactured being with a fixed lifespan who developed genuine emotions — is widely regarded as one of the most emotionally resonant character arcs in the Final Fantasy franchise. Market price ($3.91) is elevated for a Hero-tier card at this rarity tier, reflecting sustained community demand driven by character attachment rather than pure competitive utility.
sources: TCGPlayer market price data as of 2026-05-19
